content.problemStatement:
You are given an integer NNN. You have to count the number of distinct tuples (A,B,C,D)(A, B, C, D)(A,B,C,D) with 1≤A,B,C,D≤N1 \leq A, B, C, D \leq N1≤A,B,C,D≤N such that A⋅B=C⋅DA \cdot B = C \cdot DA⋅B=C⋅D.

Two tuples, say (A,B,C,D)(A, B, C, D)(A,B,C,D) and (E,F,G,H)(E, F, G, H)(E,F,G,H) are considered to be different if **at least** one of the following conditions hold true:


A≠EA \neq EA=E
B≠FB \neq FB=F
C≠GC \neq GC=G
D≠HD \neq HD=H.

Input Format

First line will contain TTT, number of testcases. Then the testcases follow.
Each testcase contains a single integer on a new line - NNN, as described in the problem statement


Output Format
For each testcase, output the answer on a new line - the number of tuples as described in the problem statement.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Test case 111:
Following tuples satisfy the given condition:

(1, 1, 1, 1)
(1, 2, 2, 1)
(1, 3, 3, 1)
(2, 1, 1, 2)
(2, 2, 2, 2)
(2, 3, 3, 2)
(3, 1, 1, 3)
(3, 2, 2, 3)
(3, 3, 3, 3)
(1, 2, 1, 2)
(2, 1, 2, 1)
(1, 3, 1, 3)
(3, 1, 3, 1)
(2, 3, 2, 3)
(3, 2, 3, 2)

A⋅B=C⋅DA \cdot B = C \cdot DA⋅B=C⋅D holds true for all these tuples.
